title: The 1925 Photomaton: 25 Cents to $1,000,000
desc:
In 1925, Siberian-born immigrant Anatol Josepho patented the Photomaton: the first fully automatic photo booth. A quarter bought a strip of 8 photos, developed in about 8 minutes with no photographer. TIME counted 280,000 customers at his Broadway studio in the first six months. In March 1927 he sold the North American rights for $1,000,000 — roughly $18 million today — plus royalties, and pledged half to charity. The company that bought it even put future president Franklin D. Roosevelt on its board. Machines that earn while you sleep are a century old.

#BrokeToBuilt #history #passiveincome #invention
